feed.title: Your site title
feed.subtitle: A short description of the site, used in the meta description tag and as the default page description.

entry.title: Podcast title
entry.summary: What you reach for it for. Status is freeform — 'Subscribed', 'Seasonal', 'Occasional'.

entry.title: Roma Leather, Fawn Tan
entry.summary: A placeholder product entry. Replace this with a short note on why this object earned a spot on the list.

entry.title: What you're reading now
entry.summary: A sentence or two on what made you pick it up. Appears on the cover and on the index.

entry.title: Something on the to-read pile
entry.summary: A line about why it's queued — borrowed, recommended, found in a charity shop.

entry.title: Something you finished recently
entry.summary: A short opinion now that you've read it. The 'Finished, recently' group on /reading/ is for the most recent reads only.

entry.title: Your first essay
entry.summary: A short sentence that previews the piece — appears under the title on the index page and on the reader.

entry.title: Your first study
entry.summary: Studies are shorter than essays — typically a single investigated question with a small data set or a focused observation.

entry.title: Your first project
entry.summary: A short blurb that previews the project — appears on the projects index card and on the case-study reader.

entry.title: Album title
entry.summary: What this record does for you in one or two sentences. The tag is freeform — 'Current rotation', 'Grief record', 'Formative'.

entry.title: Film title
entry.summary: What you took away from it. Re-watches go in the note as well.

entry.title: A novel
entry.summary: What it does to you. Notes can be one sentence; resist the urge to summarise.

entry.title: A book about craft
entry.summary: Why it earned a permanent spot. /bookshelf/ groups by section — match the section value across files to put books in the same group.

entry.title: A book about working
entry.summary: What it teaches. Sections render in the order they first appear in the file numbering, so 01- determines section order.

entry.title: Your second note
entry.summary: Add as many MDX files as you like to src/content/notes/ . They render in file order — keep the numeric 01- , 02- prefix and the most recent at the bottom (or invert the convention; build.js sorts by filename).

entry.title: Your first note
entry.summary: A note is a short fragment — usually one paragraph, sometimes two. The first paragraph becomes the lede on the reader page; subsequent paragraphs become the afterthought. Notes are grouped by month: on /notes/ and surface as the four most recent on the homepage's "From the…

entry.title: A book of essays
entry.summary: Why it stays on the shelf. Older books are welcome — the year is just for context.
